page_content: Abstract thinking skills requirement: Select one of the following: BIOM301 Introduction to Biometrics BMGT230 Business Statistics CCJS200 Statistics for Criminology and Criminal Justice ECON230 Applied Economic Statistics ECON321 Economic Statistics EDMS451 Introduction to Educational Statistics GEOG306 Introduction to Quantitative Methods for the Geographical Environmental Sciences GVPT422 Quantitative Political Analysis INST314 Statistics for Information Science JOUR405 Breaking News With Numbers: Statistics for Journalists PSYC200 Statistical Methods in Psychology SOCY201 Introductory Statistics for Sociology STAT400 Applied Probability and Statistics I (or a more advanced statistics course.) Select a minimum of 6 credits through one or a combination of the following options: 1 Language 2 Math/Statistics/Computer Science 3 Public Speaking: Select one of the following: COMM100 COMM107 Oral Communication: Principles and Practices COMM200 Critical Thinking and Speaking COMM230 Argumentation and Debate History: HIST200 Interpreting American History: Beginnings to 1877 or HIST201 Interpreting American History: From 1865 to the Present Behavioral or Social Science: Select one of the following: ANTH260 Introduction to Sociocultural Anthropology and Linguistics PSYC100 Introduction to Psychology or PSYC221 Social Psychology
